Usage: gs branch (b) comment (cmt) add [<anchor>] [flags]

Post an inline comment immediately

Posts a comment immediately on the change request for the current branch.
The anchor argument controls the scope:

    file.go:42       line-scope: anchored to that line
    file.go:42-50    line-scope multi-line range
    file.go          file-scope: anchored to the file
    (empty) + --pr   pr-scope: not anchored to any file

If no message is given with -m, an editor is opened.

Use --respond to reply to an existing thread instead of starting a new one.

Arguments:
  [<anchor>]    What to anchor the comment to: file.go:42 for a line, file.go
                for a file, or empty for the PR.

Flags:
  -m, --message=MSG          Comment body. Opens editor if not provided.
      --pr                   Post a PR-level comment with no file or line
                             anchor.
      --respond=THREAD_ID    Thread ID to reply to instead of starting a new
                             thread.
  -b, --branch=BRANCH        Branch to add comment for. Defaults to current
                             branch.

Global Flags:
  -h, --help           Show help for the command
      --version        Print version information and quit
  -v, --verbose        Enable verbose output ($GIT_SPICE_VERBOSE)
  -C, --dir=DIR        Change to DIR before doing anything
      --[no-]prompt    Whether to prompt for missing information
